name: Enciso Dinosaur Trail
name_original: Ruta de los Dinosaurios — circular desde Enciso
description: Hike the easy 3.8-mile Route of the Dinosaurs from Enciso, exploring fossilized footprints and life-size dinosaur reproductions.
region_intro: Hiking around Enciso, located in La Rioja, Spain, features a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and significant paleontological sites. The region is known for its dinosaur footprints, offering unique geological interest alongside varied terrain. The area provides a range of hiking trails suitable for different experience levels, from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Enciso The most popular hiking route is Enciso Dinosaur Trail, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This easy hike near Enciso features well-preserved dinosaur footprints. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña del Castillo and Cidacos Greenway Loop, a moderate 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This route combines historical castle ruins with a scenic walk along the Cidacos river valley. Local hikers also love the Ascent to Peñalmonte — circular from Arnedillo, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through mountain terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Enciso is defined by dinosaur footprint sites, river valleys, and varied hill and mountain ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy hikes near Enciso to more challenging routes. The routes in Enciso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Enciso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Enciso?
A: Enciso offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from gentle riverside paths to more demanding mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Enciso?
A: Yes, Enciso has several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201097977/enciso-dinosaur-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enciso Dinosaur Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.8-mile (6.1 km) route known for its well-preserved dinosaur footprints, offering an engaging experience for all ages.

Q: What are some moderate hiking options around Enciso?
A: For those seeking a moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199657133/pena-del-castillo-and-cidacos-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Castillo and Cidacos Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice. This 4.3-mile (6.9 km) path combines historical castle ruins with a scenic walk along the Cidacos river valley. Another excellent moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200857406/ascent-to-penalmonte-circular-from-arnedillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Peñalmonte — circular from Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.5 miles (12.1 km) through mountain terrain.

Q: Where can I find more challenging hikes in the Enciso area?
A: For a more strenuous adventure, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207297117/peredillo-y-los-cabezas-circular-desde-munilla-por-gr-93" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peredillo y Los Cabezas — circular desde Munilla por GR 93&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 12.3-mile (19.8 km) trail offers significant elevation changes and takes around 5 hours 45 minutes to complete, providing a rewarding experience for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Enciso?
A: Yes, many routes in Enciso are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200958076/pena-isasa-loop-from-prejano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa Loop from Préjano&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a 7.9-mile (12.7 km) moderate circular hike with impressive views.

Q: What unique natural attractions can I see while hiking around Enciso?
A: Enciso is renowned for its paleontological sites. Beyond the dinosaur footprints, you can explore natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5824640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barranco Perdido Paleo Adventure Park&lt;/a&gt;, and the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Cliffs of Herce&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas del Ajedrezado of Santa Eulalia Somera&lt;/a&gt; also offer an interesting natural feature.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles to explore near Enciso's trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5565284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herce Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The historic village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yanguas&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable nearby attraction, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Enciso?
A: The best time to hike in Enciso is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant, with wildflowers in spring and colorful foliage in autumn. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended then.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Enciso?
A: The hiking trails around Enciso are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the unique combination of natural beauty and paleontological interest, especially the dinosaur footprints, as well as the varied terrain that offers something for every ability level.

Q: Is parking generally available at Enciso trailheads?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, Enciso and its surrounding villages typically offer designated parking areas for visitors. For popular routes, it's advisable to arrive earlier in the day, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Enciso?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Enciso, provided they are kept on a leash and owners clean up after them. It's always a good idea to check local signage at specific trailheads for any particular restrictions, especially in nature reserves or protected areas.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Enciso during the summer?
A: During summer, Enciso can experience warm temperatures. It's recommended to wear lightweight, breathable clothing, a hat, and sunglasses. Always carry plenty of water, and apply sunscreen. Sturdy hiking shoes are essential for navigating varied terrain, including rocky sections on some trails.
tips: Enciso has been declared a Biosphere Reserve by UNESCO because 3,000 dinosaur footprints have been found here. This paleo-adventure park shows you interactively how these prehistoric animals lived millions of years ago. It also has swimming pools, geysers, adventure circuits, a climbing wall, a restaurant and two cafeterias.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The Route of the Dinosaurs — circular from Enciso is generally estimated to take around 1 hour and 50 minutes to complete. This duration is based on its approximately 6-7 kilometer length and easy difficulty, making it suitable for a leisurely pace.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children or beginner hikers?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is considered to be of low difficulty and is highly recommended for families, including those with children. Its interactive nature, with dinosaur reproductions and educational panels, makes it an engaging experience for all ages.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Route of the Dinosaurs?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses the natural landscape of La Rioja, featuring wide dirt paths and some sections along roads. While mostly easy, you may encounter some ascents, descents, and even sections with stairs. You'll enjoy views of the Rio Cidacos valley throughout.

faqs.question: What are the main attractions or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary attractions are the abundant fossilized dinosaur footprints (ichnites) and life-size dinosaur reproductions. You'll visit significant sites like Valdecevillo archaeological site, La Senoba archaeological site, and Virgen del Campo, where you can see hundreds of well-preserved footprints and dinosaur models. Informative panels provide details about the ancient creatures.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While enjoyable year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for hiking in La Rioja, with milder temperatures. Summer can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon start is advisable if visiting during those months.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Enciso. Specific parking information is not detailed, but as it's a circular route from the town, parking would typically be available within Enciso itself, near the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Dinosaurs — circular from Enciso dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like deer and to respect other hikers and the paleontological sites.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to hike the Route of the Dinosaurs itself. The trail is located within the Reserva de la Biosfera de los Valles del Leza, Jubera, Cidacos y Alhama, which typically allows free access to its natural areas, though specific regulations for activities should always be observed.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of other official trails. You will find parts of the Sierras Riojanas, Camino de Garranzo-Enciso, and Puentes Tibetanos trails along this route.

faqs.question: What facilities are available near the trailhead, such as cafes or accommodation?
faqs.answer: Enciso, as the starting point, offers various facilities. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options directly in the town. Additionally, the nearby El Barranco Perdido Paleo Adventure Park provides further dinosaur-themed attractions and amenities.

waypoints.name: El Barranco Perdido Paleo Adventure Park
